panels.description: HTTP response status codes over time. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: HTTP Response Codes

panels.description: HTTP request latency percentiles. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: HTTP Latency

panels.description: Outgoing gRPC calls from multigateway to multipooler. Status code 0 = OK. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: gRPC Client Response Codes

panels.description: Latency for multigateway -> multipooler gRPC calls. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: gRPC Client Latency

panels.description: Query duration percentiles across all gateway queries.
panels.title: Query Duration

panels.description: Query error rate by error type and source.
panels.title: Query Errors

panels.description: Query rate per table. Identifies hot tables in the workload.
panels.title: Queries per Table

panels.description: Number of rows returned by queries. Useful for detecting unexpected large result sets.
panels.title: Rows Returned

panels.description: Per-query performance breakdown over the selected time range. Each numeric column is a sparkline of that metric over time; the % of runtime bar shows how much total gateway time this query shape consumed.
panels.title: Per-Query Performance

panels.description: Same data as 'Top Queries by p99 Latency' but the legend shows the normalized SQL instead of the fingerprint hash. Joined via mg_gateway_query_info using PromQL group_left.

targets.expr: topk(10, histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, query_fingerprint) (rate(mg_gateway_query_duration_seconds_bucket{job="multigateway"}[$__rate_interval])))) * on (query_fingerprint) group_left(query_normalized_sql) max by (query_fingerprint, query_normalized_sql) (mg_gateway_query_info{job="multigateway"})

panels.title: Top Queries by p99 Latency (by SQL)

panels.description: Transaction duration percentiles (BEGIN to COMMIT/ROLLBACK).
panels.title: Transaction Duration

panels.description: Transaction completion rate by outcome (commit vs rollback).
panels.title: Transaction Rate

panels.description: Active gRPC notification streams (one per unique PG channel). Monitors goroutine overhead.
panels.title: Notification Streams

panels.description: Notifications dropped on the gateway side due to full subscriber channels.
panels.title: Notification Drops

panels.description: Incoming gRPC requests to multipooler. Status code 0 = OK. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: gRPC Server Response Codes

panels.description: Latency for incoming multipooler gRPC requests. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: gRPC Server Latency

panels.description: Outgoing gRPC calls from multipooler to pgctld. Status code 0 = OK. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: gRPC Client Response Codes (to pgctld)

panels.description: Latency for multipooler -> pgctld gRPC calls. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: gRPC Client Latency (to pgctld)

panels.description: PostgreSQL connection states by pool. Idle = available connections, Used = active connections. Stacked to show total pool size. Will appear once db.client.connection.count is wired up.
panels.title: PostgreSQL Connection Pool States

panels.description: Cached gRPC connections to downstream services (pgctld).
panels.title: gRPC Connection Cache Size

panels.description: gRPC connection pool efficiency. High reuse rate is good. High create rate may indicate connection churn.
panels.title: gRPC Connection Pool Operations

panels.description: Time to establish gRPC connections by dial path. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: gRPC Connection Dial Duration

panels.description: Active PG LISTEN channels and unique notification subscribers.
panels.title: PubSub Channels & Subscribers

panels.description: Notifications dropped at the multipooler due to full subscriber channels, and reconnect events.
panels.title: PubSub Drops & Reconnects

panels.description: Time from PG disconnect to successful reconnect. Proxy for notification loss window.
panels.title: Reconnect Gap Duration

panels.description: Outgoing gRPC calls from multiorch to multipooler. Status code 0 = OK. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: gRPC Client Response Codes (to multipooler)

panels.description: Latency for multiorch -> multipooler gRPC calls. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: gRPC Client Latency (to multipooler)

panels.description: Recovery actions executed by the multiorch recovery engine. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: Recovery Actions

panels.description: Recovery action duration percentiles by action type. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: Recovery Action Duration

panels.description: Active problems detected by recovery analyzer. Value of 1 indicates problem is present. Empty when no problems detected.
panels.title: Detected Problems

panels.description: Time to poll individual pooler instances for health. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: Pooler Health Check Duration

panels.description: Number of poolers tracked by recovery engine.
panels.title: Pooler Store Size

panels.description: Time to refresh cluster metadata from topology. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: Cluster Metadata Refresh Duration

panels.description: Topology lock operations by type and result. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: Topology Lock Operations

panels.description: Time to acquire/release topology locks. High duration indicates contention or etcd issues. Click on data points to view traces in Jaeger.
panels.title: Topology Lock Duration

panels.description: Client authentication attempts per second, split by outcome. success/bad_password/user_not_found/login_disabled/password_expired/protocol_error/lookup_error/internal_error/replication_role_required.
panels.title: Auth Attempts

panels.description: SCRAM handshake duration percentiles (client-first to server-final), labeled by outcome.
panels.title: SCRAM Duration

panels.description: GetAuthCredentials RPC latency observed from the gateway. p50/p95/p99. Baseline for credential-cache sizing.
panels.title: Credential Lookup Duration

panels.description: GetAuthCredentials invocations per second. Quantifies benefit of a future credential cache.
panels.title: Credential Lookup Rate

panels.description: TLS handshake duration percentiles in handleSSLRequest, labeled by outcome (success/handshake_failure/client_aborted).
panels.title: TLS Handshake Duration

panels.description: Completed TLS negotiations per second, labeled by negotiated tls_version and cipher_suite.
panels.title: TLS Connections by Version & Cipher

panels.description: Connections rejected because --pg-require-ssl=true and the client did not negotiate TLS. reason=no_sslrequest (client never asked) vs tls_disabled_by_server (server declined). Feeds the SSL-enforcement fleet validation alert (MUL-420).
panels.title: Plaintext Rejected

panels.description: SSLRequest replied with 'N' because no cert/key configured. Use before enforcement to size impact of flipping --pg-require-ssl per fleet.
panels.title: SSLRequest Declined

panels.description: Admin-pool SELECT rolpassword FROM pg_authid lookup duration percentiles. Includes admin-pool acquire + query + decode. Tail rise is the first signal of admin-pool contention.
panels.title: Credential Query Duration

panels.description: Credential-query failures per second, labeled by error_type. Baseline traffic: user_not_found, login_disabled, password_expired (policy outcomes from a successful pg_authid query). Operator issues: pool_acquire_failed, db_error.
panels.title: Credential Query Errors

panels.description: Per-phase p99 latency of gateway query handling. Decomposes mg.gateway.query.duration into parse, plan, and downstream exec so 'is it planning or execution that's slow?' is answerable. exec is the gateway's view of the pooler hop.
panels.title: Query Phase Latency (p99)

panels.description: Pooler-side query execution time (excludes gRPC framing). The gap between mg.gateway.query.exec.duration and this approximates the network/queue cost of the gateway->pooler hop.
panels.title: Pooler Query Duration

panels.description: Pooler-side query errors per second by SQLSTATE and source (backend/internal/routing/client). Distinct from gateway-side errors.
panels.title: Pooler Query Errors

panels.description: p99 time to acquire a server connection from the pool before executing, by pool_type. A rise here is pool contention rather than slow execution.
panels.title: Pool Acquire Duration (p99)

panels.description: Reserved-connection transaction outcomes per second. commit/rollback are client-driven conclusions; abort is a connection released mid-transaction (error/timeout/kill).
panels.title: Transaction Outcomes

panels.description: p99 reserved-connection transaction lifetime by outcome.
panels.title: Transaction Duration (p99)

panels.description: New PostgreSQL connection establishment rate by pool_type, and establishment failures by pool_type/reason. A high open rate with steady load indicates connection churn.
panels.title: Connection Establishment

panels.description: p99 time to establish a PostgreSQL server connection (connect + auth) by pool_type.
panels.title: Connection Setup Duration (p99)

panels.description: Replication lag observed by this pooler. 0 on a primary or before the first measurement.
panels.title: Replication Lag

panels.description: Serving-state transitions per second by from/to status (e.g. NOT_SERVING->SERVING). Spikes indicate failover/recovery churn.
panels.title: Serving State Transitions

panels.description: CPU cores consumed per component instance (rate of process CPU seconds, summed over user and system modes). The kubectl-top CPU equivalent.
panels.title: CPU Usage

panels.description: Resident set size (RSS) per component instance. The kubectl-top memory equivalent.
panels.title: Memory Usage (RSS)
